channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Berkeley changes zoning to encourage growth in innovation sector
item.description: Councilmembers voted Tuesday to permit labs studying biosafety levels 2 in mixed-use light industrial zoning areas while prohibiting BSL 3 and 4 uses in all parts of the city.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Lopez, The Mercury News

item.title: Data distilleries: CIOs turn to new efficient enterprise data platforms
item.description: In today’s data-driven world, large enterprises are aware of the immense opportunities that data and analytics present. Yet, the true value of these initiatives is in their potential to revolutionize how data is managed and utilized across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cisco strengthens AWS integration to speed troubleshooting in multicloud environments
item.description: Cisco this week said it has bolstered integrations between its own hybrid cloud offerings and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve visibility and help enterprise customers more accurately troubleshoot application availability issues across multicloud networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IT Job Market Trends: Challenges and Opportunities
item.description: Explore the latest IT job market trends, including hiring plans, job seeker challenges, highest paying certifications, and emerging technology skills.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Landlords are using AI to raise rents; California cities are leading the pushback
item.description: California and federal prosecutors have accused software company RealPage of enriching itself ”at the expense of renters who pay inflated prices.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Data- and people-driven transformation: How the Port of Auckland is navigating the future
item.description: The data and insights team at the Port of Auckland is an enterprise-level team that provides information in a systematic and consumable way to different departments in the organization so they can make data-driven decisions, see how they’re progressing toward strategic outcomes and base their decisi
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIOs’ lack of success metrics dooms many AI projects
item.description: Many organizations have launched dozens of AI proof-of-concept projects only to see a huge percentage fail, in part because CIOs don’t know whether the POCs are meeting key metrics, according to research firm IDC.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Threads is going all-in on the fediverse, and users have some questions
item.description: Threads is going deeper into the fediverse, the interconnected social platform ecosystem based on an open protocol called ActivityPub that includes apps such as Mastodon and BookWyrm.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bad data costs Americans trillions. Let’s fix it with a renewed data strategy
item.description: Nick Hart of the Data Foundation and Suzette Kent, a former federal CIO, explain how using federal data could cut waste, create jobs and own America's future. The post Bad data costs Americans trillions. Let’s fix it with a renewed data strategy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AWS upgrades its 10p10u network to handle massive AI clusters
item.description: While virtualized compute is the foundation of cloud computing, enabling all that compute to transmit data is the job of the network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The FBI now says encryption is good for you
item.description: Apple has faced an unequal battle in recent years as some lawmakers, the FBI, and regulators insist that the company create backdoors through which to access messages and other parts of its platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: A Year in Review for Music Lovers and Podcast Fanatics
item.description: Spotify Wrapped season is the best time of year for music lovers and podcast fanatics. Learn how to view your personalized recap, share it on social media, and explore alternative streaming platforms like Apple Music and YouTube Music.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Meta quietly leans on rival GPT-4 despite Zuckerberg’s bold Llama claims
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has consistently championed Meta’s Llama AI model as a leader in generative AI technology, positioning it as a strong competitor to OpenAI and Google. However, behind the scenes, Meta is complementing Llama with a rival AI model to meet its internal needs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cython tutorial: How to speed up Python
item.description: Python is a powerful programming language that is easy to learn and easy to work with, but it is not always the fastest to run—especially when you’re dealing with math or statistics.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Agencies still struggling to meet digital accessibility standards
item.description: GSA has said that limited staffing and resources are key drivers of agencies’ ongoing challenges with digital accessibility and Section 508 conformance. The post Agencies still struggling to meet digital accessibility standards first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft hit with more litigation accusing it of predatory pricing
item.description: A UK law firm on Tuesday filed what amounts to a class-action lawsuit against Microsoft, accusing it of using its market share to overcharge clients running Windows Server on AWS, Google, or Alibaba cloud offerings instead of on Microsoft Azure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Kotlin previews guard conditions in when expressions
item.description: JetBrains has released Kotlin 2.1.0, an update to the Kotlin programming language that previews guard conditions and has compiler updates offering flexibility around checks. Released November 27, Kotlin 2.1.0 can be installed as an update by following the instructions at kotlinlang.org. Kotlin 2.1.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: If you want security, start with secure products
item.description: While in these post-CrowdStrike days it is reasonable to think one of the best ways to improve endpoint security in any business is to replace Windows with Apple devices, that’s just the starting point in a serious security journey. But it’s a pretty good starting point.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rust 1.83 expands const capabilities
item.description: The Rust Team has released Rust 1.83, an update to the memory-safe and thread-safe programming language that brings expanded capabilities for code running in const contexts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 ways AI will change the software development life cycle
item.description: Considering the scaling history and trajectory of generative AI models (specifically large language models, or LLMs) specialized for coding, the software development life cycle (SDLC) is ripe for disruption.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 9 ways scammers can use your phone number to try to trick you
item.description: Scammers can wreak havoc on your life just by having your phone number. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson addresses how to avoid becoming a victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
